title: Training Language Models to Self-Correct via Reinforcement Learning
reason: LLM self-correction training method (SCoRe) improves code correctness on HumanEval benchmark.

title: Training Language Models on Synthetic Edit Sequences Improves Code Synthesis
reason: Method for iterative code synthesis via edit sequences, improving reliability and scaling on code benchmarks.

title: CraftRTL: High-quality Synthetic Data Generation for Verilog Code Models with Correct-by-Construction Non-Textual Representations and Targeted Code Repair
reason: Automated code repair framework for LLM-generated Verilog, targeting correctness via error injection and correction.

title: DR.FIX: Automatically Fixing Data Races at Industry Scale.
reason: LLMs integrated with program analysis for automated data race fixing in Go, targeting semantic correctness.

title: Let's Revise Step-by-Step: A Unified Local Search Framework for Code Generation with LLMs
reason: LLM code revision via local search; improves code generation reliability through iterative improvement.

title: ICPC-Eval: Probing the Frontiers of LLM Reasoning with Competitive Programming Contests
reason: Benchmark for LLM code reasoning with iterative repair (Refine@K), evaluating execution feedback for correctness.

title: SQLens: An End-to-End Framework for Error Detection and Correction in Text-to-SQL
reason: LLM error detection/correction for SQL generation with database & LLM signal integration

title: Pin the Tail on the Model: Blindfolded Repair of User-Flagged Failures in Text-to-Image Services
reason: Presents a model editing framework for diffusion models, a form of program repair for AI models, touching on reliability and correctness.

title: AceReason-Nemotron: Advancing Math and Code Reasoning through Reinforcement Learning
reason: Uses RL for code reasoning, with verification-based training on code tasks.

title: MigGPT: Harnessing Large Language Models for Automated Migration of Out-of-Tree Linux Kernel Patches Across Versions
reason: LLM-based automation of code migration for Linux kernel patches, addressing program correctness.

title: QiMeng-NeuComBack: Self-Evolving Translation from IR to Assembly Code
reason: LLM-based code generation (IR-to-assembly) with self-debugging to improve functional correctness.

title: Counterexample Guided Program Repair Using Zero-Shot Learning and MaxSAT-based Fault Localization
reason: Combines LLMs with formal methods (MaxSAT fault localization) for program repair, following a CEGIS loop.

title: Less Is More: Adaptive Program Repair with Bug Localization and Preference Learning
reason: Automated program repair with bug localization and consistency-focused patch generation; aligns with code debugging/reliability.
